---
title: "1.2 Net Returns and Costs"
subtitle: "PDF notes | Chapter 01. Backtesting and Automated Execution"
format:
  pdf:
    pdf-engine: xelatex
    mainfont: "NotoSerifCJK-Regular.ttc"
    mainfontoptions:
      - "Path=/usr/share/fonts/opentype/noto/"
      - "FontIndex=2"
      - "BoldFont=NotoSerifCJK-Bold.ttc"
    sansfont: "NotoSansCJK-Regular.ttc"
    sansfontoptions:
      - "Path=/usr/share/fonts/opentype/noto/"
      - "FontIndex=2"
      - "BoldFont=NotoSansCJK-Bold.ttc"
    monofont: "MapleMono-NF-CN-Regular.ttf"
    monofontoptions:
      - "Path=../../../../assets/fonts/"
      - "BoldFont=MapleMono-NF-CN-Bold.ttf"
      - "ItalicFont=MapleMono-NF-CN-Italic.ttf"
      - "BoldItalicFont=MapleMono-NF-CN-BoldItalic.ttf"
    mathfont: "latinmodern-math.otf"
    toc: true
    number-sections: true
    colorlinks: true
include-in-header:
  text: |
    \addtokomafont{disposition}{\rmfamily\bfseries}
    \addtokomafont{title}{\rmfamily\bfseries}
    \addtokomafont{author}{\rmfamily}
    \addtokomafont{date}{\rmfamily}
---

# Gross Versus Net Results

Gross returns answer one question: did the signal line up with future returns
before practical constraints?

Net returns answer the question that matters for a tradable strategy: does the
edge survive execution assumptions?

# Cost Model

A minimal cost model can start with turnover:

$$
\text{Turnover}_t = |w_t - w_{t-1}|
$$

$$
c_t = \alpha \cdot \text{Turnover}_t + \beta \cdot \sigma_t
$$

Here $\alpha$ represents direct trading costs, while $\beta \cdot \sigma_t$
stands in for liquidity-sensitive slippage or market impact.

# Figure Handling For PDF

The web version can use SVG figures for sharp browser rendering. The PDF version
should use PNG or TIFF exports from the chart-generation pipeline when final
figures are available.

Recommended export pattern:

```python
fig_pnl.savefig("figures/pnl.png", dpi=180, bbox_inches="tight")
fig_hist.savefig("figures/returns-hist.png", dpi=180, bbox_inches="tight")
```

Then include the raster files in the PDF notes:

```markdown
![](figures/pnl.png){width="90%"}
![](figures/returns-hist.png){width="80%"}
```

# Review Checklist

- Is the position shifted before returns are applied?
- Are costs applied to turnover rather than final exposure?
- Does the return distribution explain the headline Sharpe?
- Does the strategy still work after realistic capacity constraints?
